g.data-narration: Requirements validation ensures documented requirements accurately represent stakeholder needs for development.
g.text: Ensuring the RIGHT requirements for the RIGHT product

g.data-narration: Requirements validation confirms that documented requirements truly reflect stakeholder needs before development begins.
g.text: The process of ensuring documented requirements accurately
represent stakeholder needs and are suitable for development.

g.data-narration: Fixing defects during requirements validation costs 10-100 times less than fixing them during implementation or testing.
g.text: Defects found during requirements validation cost
10-100x LESS to fix than those found in implementation/testing

g.data-narration: Verification checks if we're building the product right, while validation checks if we're building the right product.
g.text: Validation vs Verification - The Key Distinction
Example: ATM - Verify PIN check works | Validate: Customer needs cash withdrawal in less than 10 seconds

g.data-narration: The four main objectives are completeness, consistency, feasibility, and testability of requirements.
Requirements review is a systematic examination by a team including analysts, developers, testers, and sometimes customers.
g.text: Systematic examination of requirements by a team of reviewers
including analysts, developers, testers, and sometimes customers.

g.data-narration: Prototyping builds working models to validate stakeholder expectations against documented requirements effectively.
g.text: Building prototypes helps validate stakeholder expectations match documented requirements.
Best for: UI requirements validation, discovering hidden user needs

g.data-narration: Creating test cases from requirements reveals ambiguities and missing information, validating requirements thoroughly.
g.text: Creating test cases from requirements helps identify ambiguities and missing information.
Requirements that can generate test cases are considered well-validated.

g.data-narration: Formal methods use mathematical specifications for rigorous analysis in safety-critical systems requiring highest validation certainty.
g.text: Mathematical specification languages (Z, VDM, Petri Nets) for rigorous analysis.
Used for safety-critical systems where highest validation certainty is required.
Delivery rate between 0-35 units per hour
Invariant: 0 is less than or equal to delivery rate is less than or equal to 35

g.data-narration: The requirements validation checklist includes completeness, consistency, feasibility, testability, clarity, traceability, and prioritization.
This ATM example shows how validation checks completeness, feasibility, testability, and clarity of requirements.
g.text: Original: ATM shall dispense cash within 10 seconds of PIN verification

g.data-narration: Key takeaways: validation differs from verification, uses four main techniques, follows a seven-point checklist, and saves 10-100x in defect costs.

svg.desc: Validating Requirements - Step-by-step educational content covering introduction, validation techniques, and key takeaways for software engineering students.
